title: The Cotton Version of Mandeville's Travels
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item aeh6691, a 2003 electronic edition whose header points to Paul Hamelius's two-volume Early English Text Society edition and says that text may have been captured in whole or in part from its 1960-1961 Oxford lithographic reprint. Reprint-only title and verso matter are encoded, but the exact physical copy and production method remain unknown. Hamelius's continuous English base text transcribes British Library Cotton MS Titus C XVI; three short lacunae and a large Chapter XV passage are explicitly supplied from Egerton MS 1982. He also compared the text with KBR MSS 10420-10425 and 11141 and George Warner's printed Anglo-French text. U-M preserves volume I's text and appendix, volume II's introduction and notes through p. 151, later Ruddy interventions, and Egerton-version cross-references, but omits the index and moves printed appendix pp. 212-217 into Chapter XV's reading order. A complete combined scan of the original 1919 and 1923 issues is cached from ORBi; no exact complete reprint scan or complete public Cotton or Egerton manuscript facsimile was verified. A complete KBR viewer surrogate for MS 10420-10425 and a complete Warner edition scan are cached as comparison evidence.
review_notes: The cataloged intellectual work is the distinct Middle English Cotton Version, not the physical Cotton codex, the multilingual Book of John Mandeville, Hamelius's edition, its reprint, or the U-M encoding.

The Cotton manuscript is cataloged as physically intact. The large textual lacuna represented by Hamelius's pp. 212-217 was inherited from a lost Defective-Version source and is not evidence of leaves now missing from Cotton MS Titus C XVI.
Exactly four passage-level supplies are assigned to Egerton: three short phrases and one large passage. The remaining forty-seven SUPPLIED elements are not assigned to Egerton.

Original print order and electronic reading order are separately recorded: the original edition runs pp. 1-211 then appendix pp. 212-217, whereas U-M places pp. 212-217 between pp. 82 and 83 inside Chapter XV.

The current U-M No Copyright-United States statement, the embedded 2003 redistribution restriction, and the pinned CME repository's software-worded Unlicense are preserved as distinct rights evidence rather than silently reconciled.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aeh6691
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item aeh6691
entities.description: A 2003 electronic edition containing reprint front matter, the Cotton-base English reading text with four Egerton supplies, Hamelius's volume II introduction and notes through p. 151, and later D. Ruddy notes and corrections. It omits volume II's index and repositions the six-page appendix inside Chapter XV.
notes: The header says the source may have been captured in whole or in part from the 1960-1961 reprint. Reprint-only title and verso matter confirm use of that publication layer, but no item-specific statement identifies a physical copy, keying, OCR, or correction workflow.

The XML has 51 SUPPLIED elements, all RESP=PH: 42 unlabeled scribal omissions, four manuscript-damage spans, one manuscript-blot span, three short SOURCE=Eg omissions, and one large SOURCE=Eg missing-leaves passage.
The XML has 76 CORR elements: 72 RESP=PH and four RESP=DR. It also has 1,133 Hamelius note DIV3 elements, thirty-two Ruddy note DIV3 elements, and nine inline PH notes in the introduction.
The header names Latin and Greek but omits the primary Middle English language.

At the opening, the XML carries HEAD TYPE=MS with value 'Maundevyll,' but visual inspection of Hamelius volume I p. 1 finds only the editor-supplied printed heading 'Mandeville's Travels.' The XML-only value is not treated as a verified manuscript title.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item acd9576, Egerton Version
entities.description: U-M's 2003 electronic Egerton-Version item. The Cotton encoding's 36 body DIV2 elements cite its prologue and thirty-four chapter identifiers through CORRESP attributes. This lightweight node models that direct electronic cross-reference only; the Egerton item's full witness and edition graph remains in its own manifest.
notes: No manuscript, source-edition, or local-repository claims for acd9576 are duplicated here.

entities.label: Oxford lithographic reprint of Hamelius's Mandeville's Travels, 1960-1961
entities.description: A two-volume lithographic reprint of Hamelius's 1919 and 1923 Early English Text Society editions, reissued by Oxford University Press in 1960 and 1961. Its reprint title and imprint matter are encoded by U-M.

identifiers.scheme: Early English Text Society Original Series

bibliographic.citation: Paul Hamelius, ed., Mandeville's Travels, Early English Text Society Original Series 153-154 (Oxford University Press, lithographic reprint, 1960-1961).
bibliographic.title: Mandeville's Travels
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society Original Series 153-154
bibliographic.pages: 2 volumes; reprint of volume I xv, 217 pages and volume II [x], 189 pages

notes: No complete public scan of the exact 1960-1961 reprint was verified; the cached ORBi PDF reproduces the original issues and lacks the reprint-only leaves visible in the XML.

entities.label: Hamelius's Mandeville's Travels, EETS OS 153-154
entities.description: Hamelius's two-volume scholarly edition. Volume I prints the Cotton-base Middle English text on pp. 1-211 and a six-page Egerton supply as an appendix on pp. 212-217. Volume II provides a foreword, preface, introduction, textual and source notes through p. 151, and an index through p. 189. Hamelius supplied three additional short passages from Egerton, punctuated the text, made limited emendations, compared two Brussels French manuscripts, and consulted Warner's printed Anglo-French text.

identifiers.scheme: Early English Text Society Original Series

bibliographic.citation: Paul Hamelius, ed., Mandeville's Travels, translated from the French of Jean d'Outremeuse, edited from MS. Cotton Titus C.XVI in the British Museum, Early English Text Society Original Series 153-154, 2 vols. (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1919 and 1923).
bibliographic.title: Mandeville's Travels
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society Original Series 153-154
bibliographic.publisher: Published for the Early English Text Society by Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co.
bibliographic.pages: volume I xv, 217 pages; volume II [x], 189 pages

notes: Hamelius's title-page claim that Jean d'Outremeuse supplied the French original is historical edition metadata, not accepted here as settled authorship.
The original source-page sequence is volume I pp. 1-211 followed by appendix pp. 212-217.

entities.label: ORBi combined scan of Hamelius's 1919 and 1923 volumes
entities.description: A complete 466-page PDF combining scans of both original-issue volumes. It was digitized through an Internet Archive workflow with University of Toronto funding and is distributed by the University of Liège's ORBi repository. It is not byte-identical to the current Internet Archive derivative and is not the 1960-1961 reprint carrier.
notes: Verified PDF SHA-256: 5eb1cc34b17469c1f09b95d7dcf52890880971ca9579526f737ab5c374e2c1fa.

entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus C XVI
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of the Cotton Middle English version, produced in England in the first quarter of the fifteenth century. Hamelius used it as the continuous base for his English text. The catalog describes the physical manuscript as intact; the version's large textual lacuna was inherited from a lost source rather than caused by leaves now absent from this codex.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century, 1400-1424

holding.notes: Held at the British Library, London; physical consultation requires a Reader Pass and letter of introduction, and no digitized content is linked from the current record.

physical_description.extent: 133 folios: an early modern paper endleaf at fol. 1 and 132 parchment leaves at fols. 2-133
physical_description.format: Manuscript codex in a 1962 British Museum binding
notes: The encoded Cotton folio milestones intend a sequence from fol. 2r through fol. 133v, with several label anomalies documented in structural evidence.

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 1982
entities.description: The unique manuscript witness of the distinct Egerton Middle English version. Hamelius used it only for four passage-level supplies in the Cotton reading text: three short omissions and one large passage associated with Chapter XV.

holding.notes: Held at the British Library, London; current record offers physical request but no digitized content.

entities.label: KBR MS 10420-10425
entities.description: A fifteenth-century heterogeneous manuscript associated with Stavelot, containing a French Mandeville text on fols. 1-148. Hamelius copied and compared this witness for his French source analysis and textual notes; it was not the base witness of his English transcription and is not identified as the Cotton translator-reviser's exemplar.

holding.notes: Biblissima records KBR as current holder and a complete digitization; the KBR stable link resolves to the public image gallery.

physical_description.extent: Mandeville fols. 1-148 within a heterogeneous compound manuscript
notes: Viewer images 0005-0300 cover the Mandeville portion from fol. 1r through fol. 148v; images outside that range include binding material and other codex contents.

entities.label: KBR complete viewer surrogate for MS 10420-10425
entities.description: A public sequence of 672 provider JPEGs covering the complete compound manuscript as published in KBR's viewer. Images 0005-0300 contain the Mandeville work portion, fols. 1-148; the cached ZIP is complete relative to the verified published viewer inventory, not a claim about unpublished captures or preservation masters.
entities.reproduction_format: 672 sequential public JPEG derivatives, approximately 800 pixels wide, bundled as a ZIP
notes: Verified bundle SHA-256: 9157ac15fa73f1df55e135355c9985970e1c99855faf7aa930aa3f6f3f586ff2.

entities.label: KBR MS 11141
entities.description: A French Mandeville manuscript dated 1463 and copied by O. Judocy at Wijk. Hamelius compared it with KBR MS 10420-10425 and cited its readings in his textual notes; it was not the English base witness and no public complete facsimile was verified.

holding.notes: Biblissima records KBR as current holder, O. Judocy as scribe, Wijk as origin, and no digital surrogate.

entities.label: Warner's 1889 edition, The Buke of John Maundeuill
entities.description: George F. Warner's bilingual edition of the Egerton English version and a reconstructed Insular French text. Hamelius refers to and compares this printed French text; the relation is routed through Warner's edition rather than asserting direct Hamelius use of Warner's Harley, Royal, Sloane, or Grenville manuscript witnesses.

bibliographic.citation: George F. Warner, ed., The Buke of John Maundeuill, Being the Travels of Sir John Mandeville, Knight, 1322-1356, Roxburghe Club Publications 119 (Westminster: Nichols and Sons, 1889).
bibliographic.title: The Buke of John Maundeuill

entities.label: Google and Internet Archive scan of Warner's 1889 edition
entities.description: A complete 360-image digital scan of Warner's 1889 volume, digitized by Google and distributed through Internet Archive with PDF and generated derivatives.
entities.reproduction_format: 360 scan images; downloadable PDF, OCR text, and generated derivatives
notes: Verified PDF SHA-256: cc523953838cc0bf9bf382eb00ed9e1dee57b127acdf5b897a6f6e60965496bc.

entities.label: The Cotton Version of the Book of John Mandeville
entities.description: The abstract Middle English version represented uniquely by Cotton MS Titus C XVI. Modern scholarship describes it as based on an English Defective-Version translation and expanded under the influence of the Insular French Version between 1400 and 1425.

entities.label: Lost Defective-Version source behind the Cotton Version
entities.description: A lost English manuscript or textual intermediary of the Defective Version used behind the Cotton Version. Its own source exemplar lacked the French original's second quire; no surviving physical exemplar is identified.

entities.label: The Insular French Version of the Book of John Mandeville
entities.description: A French recension made in England before 1375. It influenced expansion of the English Defective text into the Cotton Version, but the reviewed evidence does not identify a particular surviving manuscript as the translator-reviser's direct source.

entities.label: The Book of John Mandeville
entities.description: The anonymous or pseudonymous fourteenth-century multilingual compilation and multiversion tradition of which the Cotton, Egerton, Defective, and Insular French versions are manifestations.

entities.label: Orsi's French Phraseological Influence in Mandeville's Travels
entities.description: Tibor Orsi's 2003 scholarly article, used to support the Cotton Version's date and its relationship to the Defective and Insular French versions. It is evidentiary scholarship, not a transmission witness.

bibliographic.citation: Tibor Orsi, 'French Phraseological Influence in Mandeville's Travels,' Verbum 5.1 (2003): 221-256.
bibliographic.title: French Phraseological Influence in Mandeville's Travels

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright
entities.description: The April 2026 revision of the Copyright Office's official duration guidance, used only for the United States rights assessment of the 1889, 1919, and 1923 publications; it is not part of textual transmission.

assertion.notes: Identity is established by UMDL identifier, title, source statement, and content; a separately downloadable current U-M source XML was not checksummed.

rights.component: Checked-in XML markup and inherited U-M transcription
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository root carries an Unlicense text explicitly framed as applying to software, while this XML embeds U-M's restrictive 2003 availability notice. The path-specific CME_from_OTA Creative Commons notice does not govern CME_phase_1-2. No reviewed statement clearly resolves rights in inherited XML markup and transcription.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item aeh6691
rights.basis: The current U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States.
notes: The embedded 2003 notice permits personal, research, and teaching copies with the notice and permits linking, but forbids redistribution by commercial or not-for-profit content providers. Whether the current statement supersedes that restriction is unresolved.

rights.component: Materials presented in U-M CME item acd9576
rights.basis: The current U-M item page states that the materials are in the public domain and applies No Copyright-United States. This lightweight record does not attempt to reconcile or import every historical source or repository term documented in acd9576's own manifest.

rights.component: Reprint-specific title, imprint, typography, and other 1960-1961 publication features
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The reprint dates are 1960 and 1961, and no item-specific permission or public-domain statement for reprint-specific matter was verified. This does not alter the separate United States assessment of Hamelius's original 1919 and 1923 edition content.

rights.component: Hamelius's original 1919 and 1923 edition text, introduction, notes, and index
rights.basis: The two original volumes were first published in 1919 and 1923. U-M currently labels the selected materials public domain in the United States; Copyright Office Circular 15A states that works published in the United States before 1931 are in the public domain and describes maximum pre-1978 terms. The circular is used together with item-specific publication evidence, not as a global rights determination.

rights.component: ORBi-distributed PDF scan and provider presentation
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and applicable copyright law
rights.basis: ORBi makes the PDF openly downloadable and its user license permits reading, downloading, copying, and distribution for lawful noncommercial use, but no item-specific open license or public-domain mark for the scan was verified. The underlying original edition has a separate United States public-domain assessment.

rights.component: Physical manuscript and any modern British Library reproductions
rights.basis: The medieval text and physical object are distinct from modern catalog text and photography. No reusable complete image set or item-specific image license was verified, and British Library access or reproduction conditions would apply to supplied images.

rights.component: Physical manuscript and any modern British Library reproductions
rights.basis: No reusable complete image set or item-specific image license was verified; British Library access and reproduction conditions would apply to supplied images.

rights.component: Physical manuscript and underlying medieval text
rights.basis: The medieval physical witness and text are distinguished from modern KBR photography. Rights and contractual conditions for the public image derivatives are recorded separately on the facsimile entity.

rights.component: KBR public JPEG derivatives and cached private research bundle
rights.basis: KBR explains that freely accessible digital items are rights-free, consented, or orphan works, but no item-specific reusable license was found. KBR's legal and reproduction pages restrict reproduction and distribution and require permission for publication or an entire work. The ignored cache is retained for private research and is not asserted redistributable.

rights.component: Physical manuscript and any modern KBR reproductions
rights.basis: No public image surrogate or item-specific reuse license was verified. KBR reproduction and publication terms would apply to any supplied photography.

rights.component: Warner's 1889 transcription, French text, apparatus, commentary, index, and published plates
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1889, Internet Archive applies the Public Domain Mark to the complete scan, and Copyright Office Circular 15A describes the applicable maximum duration for older publications.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF and generated derivatives of the Google scan
rights.basis: Internet Archive applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0 to the item; the underlying 1889 edition is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Abstract historical textual version as modeled in this manifest
rights.basis: This entity is an abstract catalog model of a medieval textual version, not a modern edition, image, or licensed digital object.

rights.component: Abstract scholarly reconstruction of a lost medieval source
rights.basis: This entity represents a reported lost medieval textual intermediary, not a modern reproduced object.
This entity is a catalog model of a medieval textual version, not a modern edition or image set.
This entity is an abstract catalog model of a medieval work tradition, not a modern edition, transcription, or reproduction.

rights.jurisdiction: Applicable copyright law and repository terms
rights.basis: The article displays a 2003 Akadémiai Kiadó copyright notice. Public download does not by itself grant redistribution or republication rights; the cached copy is private research evidence.
The circular is an official work of the United States government; it is used as legal guidance evidence and not as a substitute for item-specific rights analysis.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Cotton base text with an inherited large lacuna and several shorter omissions
editorial_practices.representation: Continuous Cotton reading text plus exactly four explicit Egerton supplies
editorial_practices.description: Hamelius chose Cotton MS Titus C XVI as base, retained its imperfect readings with limited emendation, and filled four identified omissions from Egerton MS 1982.
editorial_practices.scope: Volume I pp. 1-217, especially pp. 50, 52, 67, 82, and appendix pp. 212-217

editorial_practices.representation: Editor-supplied punctuation in the printed reading text
editorial_practices.description: Hamelius explicitly states that the edition's punctuation is his.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Cotton English text, two Brussels French witnesses, and Warner's printed Anglo-French text
editorial_practices.representation: French comparisons, variant readings, translation comments, and source notes
editorial_practices.description: Hamelius compared Cotton against KBR MSS 10420-10425 and 11141 and Warner's printed French text without making those later comparison sources the base of the English transcription.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two-volume reprint containing front matter, volume I text and appendix, volume II introduction, notes, and index
editorial_practices.representation: Front matter, complete volume I textual content, and volume II matter through p. 151; index pp. 152-189 omitted
editorial_practices.description: U-M preserves extensive edition paratext but does not encode the complete printed volume II.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Large Egerton supply printed as a six-page appendix after Cotton p. 211
editorial_practices.representation: The appendix TEXT/BODY is nested inside a SUPPLIED element in Chapter XV after p. 82 and before Cotton text resumes on p. 83
editorial_practices.description: U-M changes source-page order to place the supplied passage at its intended narrative location while retaining PB values 1:212-217.
notes: Source order is 1-211 then 212-217; encoding reading order is 1-82, 212-217, then 83-211.

editorial_practices.representation: CORRESP attributes link the prologue and chapters to U-M's Egerton-version identifiers
editorial_practices.description: U-M added cross-version pointers for navigation and comparison; these links do not change Cotton's base-witness status.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Four readings represented in CORR/@SIC and attributed by RESP=DR
editorial_practices.representation: p. 56/P56.L12 'Þat//Þat' becomes 'Þat'; p. 69/P69.L5a 'men' becomes 'then'; p. 87/P87.L12 'thay' becomes 'the day'; p. 87/P87.L20 'him' becomes 'hir'
editorial_practices.description: The encoding preserves four D. Ruddy substitutions as discrete CORR spans. This is passage-level revision evidence and does not assign Ruddy responsibility for the surrounding Cotton text.
editorial_practices.scope: BODY at P56.L12, P69.L5a, P87.L12, and P87.L20

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/aeh6691.xml, header, editorial declaration, front matter, complete text, and back matter.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT; BODY Cot.Prol and Cot.Ch.1-Cot.Ch.35; BACK
evidence.summary: Supplies the U-M item identity and 2003 date, Hamelius source, qualified reprint statement, reprint-only title matter, embedded provider terms, Cotton and Egerton source labels, later Ruddy interventions, cross-version links, and complete encoded content boundaries.

evidence.citation: Computational and direct structural inspection of General Corpus aeh6691.xml.
evidence.locator: FRONT/BODY/BACK; DIV1-DIV3; P; PB; MILESTONE; SUPPLIED; CORR; PTR; NOTE1; L; language-bearing elements
evidence.summary: Finds 388 PB markers: fifteen front, 219 body, and 154 back. Body PB values include two preliminary leaves and every volume I page 1-217 exactly once, with pp. 212-217 moved between pp. 82 and 83. Back matter includes volume II [v], vi, [vii], and pp. 1-151, omitting the index pp. 152-189. It also finds 51 PH supplies including four SOURCE=Eg passages, 76 corrections (72 PH and four DR), 1,165 note DIV3 elements, nine inline introduction notes, 1,162 unique note pointers, and 133 L elements confined to editorial back matter. Cotton folio milestones intend 2a-133b but include one extra duplicate 19a, noncanonical 'fol 54 b', '110' where the sequence indicates 110a, and '114' where it indicates 114b; seven Egerton markers 50b-53b are nested inside the large supply.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Mandeville's Travels: The Cotton Version, item aeh6691.
evidence.locator: Item information, contents, full-text access, rights statement, and stable handle
evidence.summary: Confirms the public 2003 digital item, Hamelius source, displayed front matter, thirty-five chapters, appendix content in reading order, volume II introduction and notes, stable handle, and current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States assertion.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Mandeville's Travels: The Egerton Version, item acd9576.
evidence.locator: Item information, table of contents, full-text access, rights statement, and stable handle
evidence.summary: Confirms the public 2003 Egerton-Version digital item, its prologue and thirty-four displayed chapters, stable handle, and current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement. It supports only the lightweight cross-encoding node and does not substitute for acd9576's separate lineage manifest.

evidence.citation: Paul Hamelius, ed., Mandeville's Travels, Early English Text Society Original Series 153 and 154 (London, 1919 and 1923), combined ORBi scan.
evidence.locator: Volume I title and preface; text pp. 1-211; appendix pp. 212-217; volume II foreword, preface, introduction, notes pp. 23-151, and index pp. 152-189
evidence.summary: The inspected complete scan establishes the original 1919 and 1923 publication details, Cotton base, editorial punctuation and limited emendation, all four Egerton supplies, comparison with the two Brussels witnesses and Warner's French text, original page sequence, and U-M's omissions and relocation.

evidence.citation: ORBi, University of Liège, Mandeville's travels, handle 2268/190577.
evidence.summary: Provides the official repository landing page and a freely downloadable 28,733,328-byte combined PDF. The landing description emphasizes volume I, while direct inspection confirms that the file contains both original-issue volumes.

evidence.citation: ORBi, User License.
evidence.summary: States user permissions and conditions for reading, downloading, copying, and distributing deposited material for lawful noncommercial use while preserving author moral rights and repository terms; it does not supply an item-specific open license for the scan.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Cotton MS Titus C XVI.
evidence.summary: Confirms the shelfmark and title, Middle English language, English production in 1400-1424, intact status, paper endleaf and parchment fols. 2-133, 133-folio extent, approximate dimensions, 1962 binding, physical request requirements, letter-of-introduction condition, and blank digitized-content field.

evidence.citation: British Library Reference Services enquiry form for Archives and Manuscripts.
evidence.summary: Provides the current item-specific enquiry route and physical-request form used to ask about consultation, existing photography, microfilm, complete reproduction, or imaging options when no public facsimile is linked.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Egerton MS 1982.
evidence.summary: Confirms the separate Egerton Middle English witness, fifteenth-century range, vellum support, physical-request conditions, and absence of current digitized content. Hamelius and the XML establish its four passage-supply roles.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+, KBR MS 10420-10425, entity Q269011.
evidence.summary: Confirms the current KBR holding, fifteenth-century date, heterogeneous or Stavelot context, Mandeville content on fols. 1-148, authority identifier Q269011, and complete-digitization link.

evidence.citation: KBR public image gallery for MS 10420-10425.
evidence.locator: Sequential images 0001-0672; Mandeville work portion images 0005-0300
evidence.summary: A complete URL-inventory and visual audit confirms 672 published provider JPEGs, with image 0672 present and 0673 absent. Image 0005 shows fol. 1r and the Mandeville incipit, image 0299 shows the fol. 148r explicit, and image 0300 shows fol. 148v. The cached bundle is complete relative to this public sequence.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+, KBR MS 11141, entity Q268766.
evidence.summary: Confirms the KBR shelfmark, French Mandeville content, 1463 date, scribe O. Judocy, Wijk origin, authority identifier Q268766, and absence of a listed complete digital surrogate.

evidence.citation: KBR legal, copyright, and reproduction conditions.
evidence.summary: KBR explains why freely accessible collections may be online but does not provide an item-specific reuse license for MS 10420-10425; its legal and reproduction pages restrict reproduction and distribution and require item-specific permission or agreement for publication, commercial use, or an entire work.

evidence.citation: George F. Warner, ed., The Buke of John Maundeuill, Roxburghe Club Publications 119 (Westminster, 1889), Internet Archive thebukeofjohnmaundeuill.
evidence.locator: Title; introduction pp. v-xlvi; parallel English and French text pp. 1-156; commentary pp. 157-220; index through p. 232
evidence.summary: The inspected complete edition establishes Warner's printed Anglo-French text and apparatus used by Hamelius. It also identifies Warner's own manuscript bases, which are not converted into unsupported direct Hamelius-to-witness edges.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item thebukeofjohnmaundeuill and item metadata.
evidence.summary: Identifies a Google-digitized copy uploaded on 25 January 2025, exposes the direct 59,494,131-byte PDF and derivatives, and applies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0.

evidence.citation: Tibor Orsi, 'French Phraseological Influence in Mandeville's Travels,' Verbum 5.1 (2003): 221-256.
evidence.summary: Describes the Cotton Version as an English conflation based on an English Defective Version made from a lost manuscript whose source lacked the French original's second quire, then expanded through reference to the Insular Version between 1400 and 1425. It dates the Insular Version to England before 1375 and does not identify a surviving KBR manuscript as the Cotton reviser's exemplar.

evidence.citation: The Book of John Mandeville, scholarly introduction, Middle English Texts Series Editions.
evidence.summary: Synthesizes current scholarship on unresolved or pseudonymous authorship, debated French origin and approximate date, major sources, multiversion and multilingual transmission, and the Cotton and Egerton Middle English versions. It supports treating Hamelius's Jean d'Outremeuse theory as historical scholarship rather than established fact.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.summary: States that works published in the United States before 1931 are in the public domain and describes maximum terms for older works. It is used with item-specific publication and provider evidence, not as proof of global status or first U.S. publication for the London and Westminster volumes.

evidence.citation: RightsStatements.org, No Copyright - United States 1.0.
evidence.summary: Defines a jurisdiction-specific provider statement that an item is free of copyright restrictions under United States law; it is not a license and does not resolve other jurisdictions or separate contractual restrictions.

open_questions.question: Which physical reprint copy and production workflow underlie U-M item aeh6691?
next_steps: Seek item-specific production records or contact University of Michigan Digital Content and Collections.
Retain the header's qualified 'may have been captured in whole or in part' wording until keying, OCR, correction, and carrier identity are documented.

open_questions.question: Does the British Library hold existing complete photography, microfilm, or another full reproduction of Cotton MS Titus C XVI that is not exposed publicly?
next_steps: Submit the exact shelfmark through the British Library Reference Services enquiry form.
Ask specifically about existing complete photography, legacy microfilm, image ordering, conservation limits, delivery format, and reuse permission.

open_questions.question: Which Insular French exemplar or lost intermediary supplied the Cotton Version's expansion of the English Defective text?
next_steps: Consult modern critical editions and stemmatic studies beyond the reviewed Orsi summary.
Do not promote Hamelius's two KBR comparison witnesses or Warner's printed text into direct medieval source exemplars without evidence.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement supersede the redistribution restriction embedded in the 2003 XML, and how does the CME root Unlicense apply to phase 1-2 XML data?
next_steps: Ask U-M for an item-specific interpretation of the current statement and historical availability notice.
Ask the CME repository owner whether the software-worded root Unlicense is intended to license inherited XML transcriptions and markup.
Until clarified, preserve the public-domain copyright assertion separately from provider and repository terms.

open_questions.question: What item-specific reuse license, if any, governs KBR's public JPEG derivatives for MS 10420-10425?
next_steps: Request written item-specific terms from KBR before publication, redistribution, or whole-work reuse.
Continue treating the cached 672-image bundle as private research evidence only.
